metadata.name: committed_2016_sigmoid_midecon
metadata.description:
Maps the sigmoid oracle's econ-switching behavior at mid economic support (econ=0.55). Companion to committed_2016_sigmoid (same committed_split grid, econ=0.78, sigmoid).
RESEARCH QUESTION: committed_2016_sigmoid showed that sigmoid enables full econ switching (56.7%→100%) when econ=0.78 and committed_split≥0.30, yielding peak_gap=40.5% and full_switch at t=7265s. But sigmoid_2016_retarget/results_sigmoid_v2 with econ=0.35–0.70 and committed_split=0.35 showed no econ switching (peak_gap=21.6%). Is econ=0.78 uniquely required for the sigmoid econ-switching effect, or does econ=0.55 also trigger it?
MECHANISM: The sigmoid-enabled econ switching requires a sustained price gap above the effective switching threshold (~18%). With econ=0.78, the stronger econ_f component (even at 1 node) combines with sigmoid amplification to push peak_gap to ~40%. At econ=0.55, the econ_f contribution is weaker — the combined signal may or may not sustain above threshold. If no econ switching occurs at econ=0.55, this establishes an econ dimension to the sigmoid switching boundary (between 0.55 and 0.78).
PRIOR CONTEXT: - committed_2016_sigmoid (econ=0.78, sigmoid): full_switch, peak_gap=40.5%, switch_t=7265s - committed_2016_mid_econ (econ=0.55, baseline): no_switch, peak_gap=16.1% — same as high econ - sigmoid_2016_retarget/results_sigmoid_v2 (econ=0.35–0.70, committed=0.35): no_switch, 21.6% - Effective econ switching threshold: ~18% (threshold=0.10 * (1 + 0.40*2.0)) - The 21.6% gap in sigmoid_2016_retarget marginally exceeds threshold but econ switching
  did not occur — likely insufficient gap duration to overcome inertia=0.05

EXPECTED OUTCOMES: - If econ=0.55 also triggers full_switch: sigmoid econ switching is NOT econ-level dependent
  above the quantization boundary; threshold is committed_split×oracle interaction only
- If econ=0.55 shows no_switch (peak_gap≈21–25%): econ=0.78 is special; the econ dimension
  sets the boundary for sigmoid-enabled switching, which would be a new finding for §4.6

COMMITTED_SPLIT INTERPRETATION:
  committed_split=0.20: v27=14%, v26=56%, neutral=30%
  committed_split=0.30: v27=21%, v26=49%, neutral=30%
  committed_split=0.40: v27=28%, v26=42%, neutral=30%
  committed_split=0.50: v27=35%, v26=35%, neutral=30%

Run command (4 parallel namespaces, 1 scenario each — fits in ~4h):
  kubectl create namespace sweep-sigm55-0
  kubectl create namespace sweep-sigm55-1
  kubectl create namespace sweep-sigm55-2
  kubectl create namespace sweep-sigm55-3

  python tools/sweep/3_run_sweep.py \
    --input tools/sweep/committed_2016_sigmoid_midecon/build_manifest.json \
    --scenarios sweep_0000 --namespace sweep-sigm55-0 \
    --results-dir committed_2016_sigmoid_midecon/results \
    --duration 13000 --retarget-interval 2016 --interval 2 --use-sigmoid &

  python tools/sweep/3_run_sweep.py \
    --input tools/sweep/committed_2016_sigmoid_midecon/build_manifest.json \
    --scenarios sweep_0001 --namespace sweep-sigm55-1 \
    --results-dir committed_2016_sigmoid_midecon/results \
    --duration 13000 --retarget-interval 2016 --interval 2 --use-sigmoid &

  python tools/sweep/3_run_sweep.py \
    --input tools/sweep/committed_2016_sigmoid_midecon/build_manifest.json \
    --scenarios sweep_0002 --namespace sweep-sigm55-2 \
    --results-dir committed_2016_sigmoid_midecon/results \
    --duration 13000 --retarget-interval 2016 --interval 2 --use-sigmoid &

  python tools/sweep/3_run_sweep.py \
    --input tools/sweep/committed_2016_sigmoid_midecon/build_manifest.json \
    --scenarios sweep_0003 --namespace sweep-sigm55-3 \
    --results-dir committed_2016_sigmoid_midecon/results \
    --duration 13000 --retarget-interval 2016 --interval 2 --use-sigmoid &

Wall-clock: 4 scenarios × 13,000s parallel = 13,000s ≈ 3.6h
